one_line_description: Open-source ecommerce plugin for WordPress with a built-in REST API per store.
auth_summary: Store admins generate consumer key/secret API keys (WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API) used as HTTP Basic auth credentials over HTTPS; over plain HTTP a one-legged OAuth 1.0a-style HMAC signature scheme is required. No standard OAuth2 flow.
access_notes: Fully self-serve, but per-store: each merchant generates their own consumer key/secret in wp-admin with read/write scope. There is no central developer portal or app review; the software itself is free and self-hosted.
api_surface_summary: REST API (v3) covers products, orders, customers, coupons, refunds, shipping, taxes, reports, settings, and webhooks.
agent_notes: WooCommerce now has native/official MCP integration documented in its developer docs (built on the WordPress Abilities system with a local proxy), plus many community MCP servers. Main operational caveat for Composio: credentials are per-store (each user must paste their own site URL + key/secret); connection quality depends on each store's hosting/SSL setup.
